data.body:
Implemented across 4 commits (one per WU), suite green at each step:
- WU-1 (382f): removed _ensure_gc_auto_zero + all four gc.auto=0 writes; init now --unsets gc.auto + sets gc.autoDetach=true (idempotent migration on fresh + re-init). Test: gc --prune=now loses no reachable ticket data.
- WU-3 (7dc7): tickets-branch .gitattributes merge=ours for .bridge_state/* + .reconciler-*. GOTCHA the epic text omitted: merge=ours is silently ignored unless the 'ours' merge driver is configured (merge.ours.driver=true) — verified empirically (without it bindings.json conflicts). init now configures it. Test: divergent bindings.json merges without a wedge.
- WU-2 (4a7e): sync.py Path 1 now _union_merge(--allow-unrelated-histories), reusing the diverged-path conflict net; ff-adopt reset (discards nothing) unchanged. Test: unrelated-history reconverge unions both tickets, keeps local commit reachable (2-parent merge), survives gc --prune=now.
- WU-4 (2e77): docs/concurrency.md + sync.py docstring rewritten to the non-destructive-recovery safety invariant.

NOTE: one pre-existing suite failure (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_signature.py::test_foreign_key_round_trip_via_file_swap) is unrelated to this epic — confirmed it fails identically at the pre-epic baseline commit 6426ab81. All other 2450 non-integration tests pass.
